findings.issue: Task 13 context section lists `ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt` at path `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt` (a production source directory). Test files in this project live under `src/test/kotlin/`, not `src/main/kotlin/`. The tech-spec (Task 3) creates this file in `core-runtime/src/test/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt`. An agent following the context path will look in the wrong directory and conclude the file is missing or miscreated.
findings.fix: Change line 94 in task 13 from `core-runtime/src/main/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt` to `core-runtime/src/test/kotlin/app/sanctum/machina/core/registry/ModelRegistryActiveModelTest.kt`.

findings.issue: `ModelEntry` does not have an `isDownloaded` field — it has `downloadStatus: ModelDownloadStatus` with a `status: ModelDownloadStatusType` enum. Task 13's focus-area reference (section 2 under ChatIdentity) and audit checklist do not explicitly reference `isDownloaded` by name, but the tech-spec's Architecture section (How it works, line 59 and line 81) and Task 9's description both reference `ModelEntry.isDownloaded`. This boolean convenience property does not exist on the current `ModelEntry` data class. If the audit agent attempts to grep for `isDownloaded` in the registry code it will find nothing, which may produce a false finding. The Phase-3 Task 3 (ModelRegistry update) is expected to add this, but the audit task must acknowledge it is a Task-3 addition, not a pre-existing field.
findings.fix: Add a note in Task 13 Details (and/or in the tech-spec How-it-works section) that `ModelEntry.isDownloaded` is a computed property or an additional field added by Task 3 (`val isDownloaded: Boolean get() = downloadStatus.status == ModelDownloadStatusType.SUCCEEDED`), not present in the baseline `ModelEntry.kt`. The audit must check for it as a Phase-3 addition rather than a pre-existing field.

findings.issue: Task 13 audit checklist (Additional checks, focus area 6) states: "WarmupCoordinator reads AppSettings.default_model_id / last_used_model_id and passes them to registry.initialize(modelId) — not registry.initialize(modelName)". However, `ModelRegistry.initialize()` currently has the signature `suspend fun initialize(modelName: String)` (verified in `ModelRegistry.kt` line 60). The interface uses `modelName`, not `modelId`. The tech-spec does not explicitly say Task 3 renames the parameter to `modelId`. If `WarmupCoordinator` calls `registry.initialize(modelId)` but the parameter is still named `modelName`, the parameter name mismatch is confusing but not a code error (Kotlin parameter names are not part of the call site). However, the audit task should be clear about whether the interface parameter name is expected to change — or whether the audit is purely about passing the correct value (the stable HF id from AppSettings) regardless of parameter name.
findings.fix: Clarify in Task 13 audit checklist for focus area 6 whether `registry.initialize` parameter name is expected to be renamed to `modelId` by Task 3, or whether the audit only verifies that `WarmupCoordinator` passes the AppSettings `default_model_id` / `last_used_model_id` value (stable HF id) to the unchanged `initialize(modelName: String)` signature.

findings.issue: `ModelRegistry` interface does not currently have `val activeModelName: StateFlow<String?>`. Task 13 audit checklist (Additional checks line 165) states: "`ModelRegistry` interface: `val activeModelName: StateFlow<String?>` present". This is a Task-3 addition — it does not exist in the codebase yet. The audit agent must verify it was correctly added by Task 3, not that it pre-exists. The task description correctly identifies Task 3 as adding this, but the audit checklist phrasing "present" could mislead the auditor into thinking it is already there before Phase-3 tasks run.
findings.fix: Rephrase the audit checklist item to: "Verify Task 3 added `val activeModelName: StateFlow<String?>` to `ModelRegistry` interface AND implemented it in `DefaultModelRegistry`." Make explicit that this is a Phase-3 addition being verified, not a baseline check.

findings.issue: `Model.kt` does not currently have `val modelId: String`. Task 13 audit checklist (Additional checks line 166) states: "`Model.kt`: `val modelId: String` field present". The current `Model.kt` has `val name: String` but not `modelId`. Only `AllowedModel` has `val modelId: String`, and its `toModel()` function does NOT pass `modelId` to the `Model(...)` constructor. Task 2 is supposed to add `modelId: String` to `Model` and plumb it through `AllowedModel.toModel()`. The audit must verify this Task-2 change, not assume it pre-exists.
findings.fix: Rephrase the audit checklist item to: "Verify Task 2 added `val modelId: String` to `Model.kt` AND wired it through `AllowedModel.toModel()`." The auditor should grep `ModelAllowlist.kt` for `modelId = modelId` (or similar) in the `return Model(...)` block.

findings.issue: `app_settings.proto` does not currently contain `optional string default_model_id = 2`, `optional string last_used_model_id = 3`, or `optional bool settings_keys_migrated = 4`. The proto only has `map<string, PerModelSettings> per_model_overrides = 1`. Task 13 audit checklist (Additional checks) checks for these fields as if they should be present. They are added by Task 2 and must be verified as Task-2 additions.
findings.fix: Same fix pattern: audit checklist should explicitly frame these as "verify Task 2 added" items, not baseline-present checks. This avoids the auditor incorrectly flagging them as missing if Task 2 ran correctly.

findings.issue: Task 13 describes reading `decisions.md` in step 1 to understand what was actually implemented. At the time Task 13 runs (Wave 7, after all prior tasks complete), `decisions.md` should have entries for Tasks 0–12. However, `decisions.md` is currently empty (only the template comment). The task correctly positions reading `decisions.md` as a runtime step — agents running Task 0–12 will populate it. This is a minor scheduling note, not a correctness issue, but if `decisions.md` is sparse (tasks not writing entries), the auditor loses important context about deviations.
findings.fix: No change needed to the task. Consider adding a note: "If decisions.md has fewer than 12 entries, check with the team lead — some tasks may not have recorded their completion notes."

findings.issue: Task 13 says in step 5: "Record the audit report in `decisions.md` under Task 13 (summary + link to report JSON if saved to logs)" but never specifies a concrete output path for the report JSON (unlike Task 14 which specifies `work/phase-3-history/logs/working/task-14/security-audit-report.md`). The post-completion checklist says "Write audit report entry in decisions.md" with no separate file path. This creates an ambiguity: where is the full structured audit report stored?
findings.fix: Add an explicit output file path such as `work/phase-3-history/logs/working/task-13/code-audit-report.md` analogous to Task 14's convention. Update the post-completion checklist accordingly.

findings.issue: Task 14 context section lines 73–75 use relative paths starting with `../../.claude/...` for project-knowledge files. From the task file location (`work/phase-3-history/tasks/14.md`), `../../` resolves to `work/` not the repository root, making these paths incorrect as filesystem relative references. The absolute paths resolve correctly when interpreted relative to `feature_path` (`work/phase-3-history/`), but a literal filesystem resolution from `tasks/14.md` would fail. This could confuse an agent attempting to navigate the relative links.
findings.fix: Use absolute paths for project-knowledge references in Task 14 context section: `C:/AI-WORK/PhoneWrap/.claude/skills/project-knowledge/references/project.md` etc., as Task 13 does (lines 73–75 in task 13 use absolute paths correctly). Or clarify that relative links are relative to `feature_path`, not to the task file itself.

findings.issue: Task 12's automated verification commands (lines 86–103) grep for `project_files` expecting a count of 1 after the update ("only the not-created-in-v1 note remains"). However, the current `architecture.md` also contains `project_files.project_id` in the Indexes section (line 266) and the `project_files` FK entry in Key Constraints (line 265). After Task 12's edits per its own What-to-do step 3, both of these should be removed, leaving only the replacement note. The grep count check (`-c`) counting lines with 'project_files' would then return 1 only if exactly one mention remains. If the task executor misses removing one of the three locations, the count would return 2 and the check would correctly fail — so the check is functional. This is informational only.
findings.fix: No change required — the grep count of 1 is a correct gate. Minor improvement: add a comment to the verification script explaining which single mention is expected to remain (the Phase 4 note).
